revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県桶川市篠津58 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 01 |緯度秒 = 55.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 13.0 |祭神 = 豊葦建姫命、倉稲魂命 |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = 式内社
旧村社 |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} 多気比売神社（かわすくねじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある豊葦建姫命を祭神とする神社。武蔵国足立郡の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 ==祭神== 地名の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて篠津沼という大きな沼があった。
田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） ==由緒== [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。
[[明治]]元年（1868）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称。
神仏分離により別当金剛寺の僧が金田に改名、神職となり昭和20年まで神社を管理した。 [[明治]]6年（1874）に[[村社]]に列する。
[[明治]]20年（1888）に神職を加納の桜井家が引き継ぐ。
[[明治]]40年に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀する。
==祭事== *祈年祭（2月11日） *例大祭（3月15日） *ふせぎ（4月1日） *新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） ==文化財== ===市指定天然記念物=== *多気比売神社の大シイ File:Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|thumb|多気比売神社の大シイ ==境内社== 3地区に分かれてお祀りされていた篠津の神社を合祀。 *天神神社 *稲荷神社 *三峰神社 File:Tenman-jinja.jpg|thumb|天満神社 File:Inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|稲荷神社 File:Yakumo-jinja.jpg|thumb|八雲神社 ==境内== File:Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|鳥居 File:Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|thumb|社殿 File:Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|thumb|社殿の扁額 File:Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|thumb|力石 ==交通== JR東日本 高崎線 桶川駅東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分
バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 {{デフォルトソート:かわすくねしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵野国の式内社]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県桶川市篠津58 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 01 |緯度秒 = 55.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 13.0 |祭神 = 豊葦建姫命、倉稲魂命 |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = 式内社
旧村社 |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} 多気比売神社（かわすくねじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある豊葦建姫命を祭神とする神社。武蔵国足立郡の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 ==祭神== 地名の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて篠津沼という大きな沼があった。
田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） ==由緒== [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。
[[明治]]元年（1868）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称。
神仏分離により別当金剛寺の僧が金田に改名、神職となり昭和20年まで神社を管理した。 [[明治]]6年（1874）に[[村社]]に列する。
[[明治]]20年（1888）に神職を加納の桜井家が引き継ぐ。
[[明治]]40年に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀する。

旧村社 |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} 多気比売神社（かわすくねじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある豊葦建姫命を祭神とする神社。武蔵国足立郡の式内小社。旧社格は村社。 ==由緒== [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。
[[明治]]元年（1868）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称。
神仏分離により別当金剛寺の僧が金田に改名、神職となり昭和20年まで神社を管理した。
[[明治]]6年（1874）に[[村社]]に列する。
[[明治]]20年（1888）に神職を加納の桜井家が引き継ぐ。
[[明治]]40年に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀する。
==祭神== 地名の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて篠津沼という大きな沼があった。
田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） ==祭事== *祈年祭（2月11日） *例大祭（3月15日） *ふせぎ（4月1日） *新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） ==文化財== ===市指定天然記念物=== *多気比売神社の大シイ File:Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|thumb|多気比売神社の大シイ ==境内社== 3地区に分かれてお祀りされていた篠津の神社を合祀。 *天神神社 *稲荷神社 *三峰神社 File:Tenman-jinja.jpg|thumb|天満神社 File:Inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|稲荷神社 File:Yakumo-jinja.jpg|thumb|八雲神社 ==境内== File:Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|鳥居 File:Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|thumb|社殿 File:Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|thumb|社殿の扁額 File:Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|thumb|力石 ==交通== JR東日本 高崎線 桶川駅東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分
バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 {{デフォルトソート:かわすくねしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧郷社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

{{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|280px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県桶川市篠津58 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 01 |緯度秒 = 55.6 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 13.0 |祭神 = 豊葦建姫命、倉稲魂命 |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = 式内社
旧村社 |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の式内小社で、旧社格は村社。 ==由緒== [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が金田に改名して神職となり、明治20年（1888年）まで神社を管理した。明治6年（1874年）に[[村社]]に列し、明治20年に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。明治40年（1908年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 ==祭神== 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） ==祭事== *祈年祭（2月11日） *例大祭（3月15日） *ふせぎ（4月1日） *新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） ==文化財== ===市指定天然記念物=== *多気比売神社の大シイ File:Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|thumb|多気比売神社の大シイ ==境内社== 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 *天神神社 *稲荷神社 *三峰神社 File:Tenman-jinja.jpg|thumb|天満神社 File:Inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|稲荷神社 File:Yakumo-jinja.jpg|thumb|八雲神社 ==境内== File:Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|鳥居 File:Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|thumb|社殿 File:Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|thumb|社殿の扁額 File:Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|thumb|力石 ==交通== * JR東日本 高崎線 桶川駅東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==由緒== [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、明治20年（1888年）まで神社を管理した。明治6年（1874年）に[[村社]]に列し、明治20年に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。明治40年（1908年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 ==祭神== 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 *[[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） *[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） ==祭事== *祈年祭（2月11日） *例大祭（3月15日） *ふせぎ（4月1日） *新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） ==文化財== ===市指定天然記念物=== *多気比売神社の大シイ File:Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|thumb|多気比売神社の大シイ ==境内社== 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 *天神神社 *稲荷神社 *三峰神社 File:Tenman-jinja.jpg|thumb|天満神社 File:Inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|稲荷神社 File:Yakumo-jinja.jpg|thumb|八雲神社 ==境内== File:Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|鳥居 File:Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|thumb|社殿 File:Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|thumb|社殿の扁額 File:Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|thumb|力石 ==交通== * JR東日本 高崎線 桶川駅東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

revision.comment: /* 由緒 */ [[Wikipedia:表記ガイド#西暦・和暦]]「（新暦採用後の1873年〈明治6年〉1月1日以後）原則として西暦を先に表記します」に基づき、「明治20年（1888年）」等→「1887年（明治20年）」等に修正（西暦年が1年ずれているのを訂正）。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]篠津58 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]、[[倉稲魂命]] |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に[[村社]]に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 == 祭神 == 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） == 文化財 == === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ == 境内社 == 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 == 境内 == Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 == 交通 == * JR東日本[[高崎線]][[桶川駅]]東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[武蔵国の式内社一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Takehime Shrine'''|多気比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takehime Shrine'''|多気比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Takehime Shrine'''|多気比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Takehime Shrine'''|多気比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]篠津58 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]、[[倉稲魂命]] |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に[[村社]]に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 == 祭神 == 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） == 文化財 == === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ == 境内社 == 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 == 境内 == Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 == 交通 == * JR東日本[[高崎線]][[桶川駅]]東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[武蔵国の式内社一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に[[村社]]に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 == 祭神 == 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） == 文化財 == === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ == 境内社 == 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 == 境内 == Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 == 交通 == * JR東日本[[高崎線]][[桶川駅]]東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[武蔵国の式内社一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|WD=Q11546334}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Okegawa, Saitama|lt=Okegawa|ar|أوكيغاوا (سايتاما)|azb|اوکهقاوا، سایتاما|ceb|Okegawa-shi|ce|Окегава|de|Okegawa|en|Okegawa, Saitama|eo|Okegaŭa|es|Okegawa|fa|اوکه‌گاوا، سایتاما|fi|Okegawa|fr|Okegawa|gl|Okegawa|id|Okegawa, Saitama|it|Okegawa|ja|桶川市|javoyage|桶川市|ko|오케가와시|mg|Okegawa|mzn|اوکه‌گاوا، سایتاما|nl|Okegawa|pl|Okegawa|pt|Okegawa|ro|Okegawa|ru|Окегава|sv|Okegawa|tg|Окегава|th|โอเกงาวะ|tl|Okegawa, (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733}} ==References== [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Q brackets]] {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] {{translated page|ja|多気比売神社|version=107001558|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]篠津58 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]、[[倉稲魂命]] |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = [[式内社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に[[村社]]に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 == 祭神 == 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） == 文化財 == === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ == 境内社 == 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 == 境内 == Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 == 交通 == * JR東日本[[高崎線]][[桶川駅]]東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[武蔵国の式内社一覧]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

旧[[村社]] |創建 = （伝）[[安寧天皇]]の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = [[権現造]] |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[桶川市]]の[[元荒川]]沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。[[武蔵国]][[足立郡]]の[[式内社|式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 由緒 == [[安寧天皇]]の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。[[明治]]元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、[[神仏分離]]に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に[[村社]]に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の[[稲荷社]]を合祀した。 == 祭神 == 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * [[トヨタマヒメ|豊葦建姫命]]（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * [[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]（うかのみたまのみこと） == 祭事 == * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） == 文化財 == === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ == 境内社 == 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 == 境内 == Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 == 交通 == * JR東日本[[高崎線]][[桶川駅]]東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[武蔵国の式内社一覧]] ==End Japanese== [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Takehime Shrine'''|多気比売神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 多気比売神社 |画像 = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|桶川市|Q570333}}篠津58 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊葦建姫命}}、{{jalink|倉稲魂命}} |神体 = 十二単衣冠の座像 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年） |宮司 = |本殿 = {{jalink|権現造|Q3155268}} |別名 = 姫宮大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 3月15日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=多気比売
神社|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''多気比売神社'''（たけひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|埼玉県|Q128186}}{{jalink|桶川市|Q570333}}の{{jalink|元荒川|Q11388047}}沿いにある神社。祭神は豊葦建姫命。{{jalink|武蔵国|Q907398}}{{jalink|足立郡|Q11637033}}の{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|安寧天皇|Q313345}}の時代（紀元前567年 - 紀元前510年）に創建されたと伝えられる。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（1868年）に社名を「姫宮社」から「多気比売神社」に改称し、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}に伴い別当金剛寺の僧が還俗して神職となり、1887年（明治20年）まで神社を管理した。1873年（明治6年）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列し、1887年（明治20年）に神職が金田家から加納の桜井家へ交代した。1907年（明治40年）に大字五丁台字上耕地の{{jalink|稲荷社}}を合祀した。 === 祭神 === 神社が鎮座する篠津の「篠」は竹、「津」は船着き場を意味し、かつて同地には篠津沼という大きな沼があった。田園が広がる地域ということもあり、稲を神格化した女神を祀ったといわれている。 * {{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊葦建姫命}}（とよあしたけひめのみこと） * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}}（うかのみたまのみこと） === 祭事 === * 祈年祭（2月11日） * 例大祭（3月15日） * ふせぎ（4月1日） * 新嘗祭（11月13日） 社頭板「多気比売神社 御由緒」より（2019年7月22日） === 文化財 === === 市指定天然記念物 === * 多気比売神社の大シイ Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|多気比売神社の大シイ === 境内社 === 3地区に分かれて祀られていた篠津の各神社を合祀している。 * 天神神社 * 稲荷神社 * 三峰神社 Tenman-jinja.jpg|天満神社 Inari-jinja.jpg|稲荷神社 Yakumo-jinja.jpg|八雲神社 === 境内 === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|鳥居 Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|社殿 Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|社殿の扁額 Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|力石 === 交通 === * JR東日本{{jalink|高崎線|Q1346657}}{{jalink|桶川駅|Q844901}}東口から 朝日バス「菖蒲車庫」行き 20分 * バス停「東部工業団地入口」下車 徒歩5分 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|武蔵国の式内社一覧|Q11546334}} {{デフォルトソート:たけひめしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}
Former {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{jalink|Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{jalink|Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047}} in {{jalink|Okegawa City|Q570333}}, {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{jalink|Adachi District|Q11637033}}, {{jalink|Musashi Province|Q907398}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was that of a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Annei|Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{jalink|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{jalink|Village Shrine|Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{jalink|Inari Shrine}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{jalink|Toyotamahime|Q2329261|Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{jalink|Okegawa Station|Q844901}} on the JR East {{jalink|Takasaki Line|Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill||lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill||lt=Inari Shrine|UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]

category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:桶川市の建築物]] [[Category:桶川市の歴史]]
