category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takehime Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] == postal code (P281) == * 363-0003https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7030005006396 == Commons category (P373) == * Takehime-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] == postal code (P281) == * 363-0003https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7030005006396 == Commons category (P373) == * Takehime-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] == postal code (P281) == * 363-0003https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7030005006396 == Commons category (P373) == * Takehime-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] == postal code (P281) == * 363-0003https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7030005006396 == Commons category (P373) == * Takehime-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Takehimeno Shrine|lt=Takehimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039733|1=Takehimeno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県桶川市篠津58', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone === Access === * From the east exit of {{ill|lt=Okegawa Station|Q844901|WD=Q844901|1=Okegawa Station}} on the JR East {{ill|lt=Takasaki Line|Q1346657|WD=Q1346657|1=Takasaki Line}}, take the Asahi Bus bound for “Shōbu Shako” for 20 minutes * Get off at the “Tōbu Industrial Park Entrance” bus stop, then walk 5 minutes === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|Q11546334|WD=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takehime Shrine (Q65734340)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] category|Category:Takehime-jinja}} [[JA:多気比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65734340}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takehime Shrine |Image = [[File:Takehime-jinja.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}} 58 Shinotsu |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Sacred object = Seated statue wearing a twelve-layered ceremonial robe and crown |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = (Traditionally) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE) |Chief priest = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Gongen-zukuri|Q3155268|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} |Other names = Himemiya Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = March 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|lat_deg=36|lat_min=1|lat_sec=55.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=34|lon_sec=13|label=Takehime
Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|1|55.6|N|139|34|13|E}}}} }} '''Takehime Shrine''' (たけひめじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located along the {{ill|lt=Moto-Arakawa River|Q11388047|WD=Q11388047|1=Moto-Arakawa River}} in {{ill|lt=Okegawa City|Q570333|WD=Q570333|1=Okegawa, Saitama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|WD=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. The enshrined deity is Toyotake-hime no Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor shrine listed in the Engishiki}} of {{ill|lt=Adachi District|Q11637033|WD=Q11637033|1=Adachi district}}, {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was that of a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === History === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Annei|Q313345|WD=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}} (567 BCE – 510 BCE). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (1868), the shrine’s name was changed from “Himemiya Shrine” to “Takehime Shrine.” With the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the monk of its affiliated temple, Kongō-ji, returned to secular life and became a Shinto priest, managing the shrine until 1887 (Meiji 20). In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and in 1887 (Meiji 20), the priesthood was transferred from the Kaneda family to the Sakurai family of Kanō. In 1907 (Meiji 40), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} located at Ōaza Gochōdai, Aza Kamikōchi, was enshrined together with it. === Enshrined deities === In the place name Shinotsu where the shrine stands, “shino” refers to bamboo and “tsu” means a landing place; in the past, there was a large marsh called Shinotsu-numa here. As the area was surrounded by farmland, it is said that a goddess who personified rice was enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Toyotamahime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotake-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (とよあしたけひめのみこと) * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのみこと) === Festivals === * Kinensai (February 11) * Annual Grand Festival (March 15) * Fusegi (April 1) * Niinamesai (November 13) From the shrine information board “Takehime Shrine: History” (July 22, 2019) === Cultural properties === === City-designated Natural Monument === * The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine Takehime-jinja ooshii.jpg|The large shii tree of Takehime Shrine === Sub-shrines === The various shrines of Shinotsu that had been worshiped separately in three districts are enshrined together. * Tenjin Shrine * Inari Shrine * Mitsumine Shrine Tenman-jinja.jpg|Tenman Shrine Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari Shrine Yakumo-jinja.jpg|Yakumo Shrine === Shrine grounds === Takehime-jinja Torii.jpg|Torii gate Takehime-jinja Shrine.jpg|Shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Flat rate.jpg|Plaque of the shrine buildings Takehime-jinja Rikiishi.jpg|Strength stone
